description_long: This is a three-story Mediterranean-style house with a hip roof covered in terracotta tiles and two prominent chimneys. The facade features a mix of stone or stucco walls, green wooden window shutters, and climbing ivy that adds a touch of natural charm. The front of the house has a set of double green doors, multiple symmetrically arranged windows, and a small overhang above the entryway. One side of the house includes a partially open balcony with a lattice railing and more ivy, while the rear view reveals a covered terrace area. The windows are mostly rectangular and arranged in neat rows, with some decorative grilles on the uppermost level. The overall appearance is rustic yet elegant, suitable for a European or Mediterranean urban neighborhood.
description: A three-story Mediterranean house with terracotta roof tiles, green shutters, stone walls, and climbing ivy, featuring a symmetrical window arrangement and a covered balcony.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main double green doors, symmetrical windows, and ivy climbing up the left side.
description_view_1: Left side view with rows of windows and a glimpse of the balcony and lattice railing, plus some ivy.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the covered terrace, balcony with lattice, and more ivy on the upper level.
description_view_3: Right side view with a green door at the base, vertical window rows, and ivy climbing up the wall.
attribute_entryway_design: Double green doors with a small overhang, stone steps, and climbing ivy for decoration.
